one_line_takeaway: A training-free, model-agnostic 'caption-and-regenerate' consistency detector for adversarial VLM inputs that hits up to 99% detection and, via randomized T2I/encoder selection plus one-time-use weight noise, stays robust under BPDA+EoT white-box adaptive attacks, though it underperforms baselines on some victims (e.g. MiniGPT-4) and costs ~15 s/image.
key_finding: Stochastic MirrorCheck detects adversarial VLM/classifier inputs with accuracy up to 99% and consistently beats 10 adapted baselines, often by a large margin, without any retraining or access to victim gradients/logits. Detection accuracy (Table 2) spans ~65-99% across victims/attacks. In the head-to-head (Table 3), on UniDiffuser/AttackVLM-Q it reaches 0.98 (>0.13 above the next best baseline) and on BLIP-2/AttackVLM-Q it reaches 0.99. Critically, it holds up under a strong white-box BPDA+EoT adaptive attack: with >=7 encoders all noise configs exceed 98%, and even at the highest OTU noise (1e-3) accuracy climbs from 71% (1 encoder) to 99% (10 encoders), evidencing that encoder diversity plus stochasticity, not gradient masking, drives robustness.
core_contribution: MirrorCheck, a training-free, model-agnostic framework for DETECTING adversarial images fed to Vision-Language Models (and image classifiers). The core idea is a 'round-trip' consistency check: run the input image through the victim model to get its text output (caption/answer/class), regenerate an image from that text with a Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion model, then compare feature-space (cosine) similarity between the original and regenerated images using pretrained image encoders. Clean inputs yield high similarity; adversarial inputs, whose caption diverges from the true image content, yield low similarity, so a threshold tau (chosen via ROC / max TPR-FPR) flags adversarial samples. The paper proposes two variants: (i) Vanilla MirrorCheck (fixed T2I model + fixed/ensemble encoders), and (ii) Stochastic MirrorCheck, which adds three defenses specifically to resist white-box adaptive attacks: randomized T2I-model selection from a zoo, random selection of n encoders from a larger pool of N, and a novel One-Time-Use (OTU) Gaussian perturbation gamma added to encoder weights each inference. Note: FGSM/PGD/BPDA/EoT/AttackVLM etc. are cited prior work used as the evaluation attacks/tooling, NOT this paper's contribution; the paper's own contributions are the detection pipeline, the stochastic/OTU defense, and the BPDA-with-learnable-Adapter adaptive-attack evaluation.
threat_model: Explicitly stated (Sec 3.1). Attacker: crafts an l-norm-bounded perturbation delta so xadv = xclean + delta makes the victim VLM/classifier output a wrong (targeted or untargeted) caption/class while keeping delta imperceptible. The attacker may be black-box OR full white-box (architecture, parameters, training data). Defender: only has black-box access to the victim model and the single input image xin (no ground-truth clean reference, no knowledge of the attack strategy); must label xin clean vs adversarial. For the robustness study the paper additionally assumes a worst-case white-box adaptive attacker with full knowledge of the victim, the entire encoder zoo, the T2I models, and the detection mechanism, whose dual goal is to fool the victim AND maximize regenerated-image similarity to evade detection.
limitations: 1) Detection quality is highly victim-dependent and sometimes LOSES to baselines: on MiniGPT-4/AttackVLM-T MirrorCheck scores only 0.66 (MC) / 0.67 (Stochastic-MC) versus 0.85 for CIDER, JailGuard and DPS (Naive is actually worse at 0.57); on Img2Prompt/AttackVLM-T Vanilla MC is 0.79 (below several baselines) and on LLaVA/OpenFlamingo it merely ties or trails multimodal baselines. 2) The adaptive-attack evaluation uses only 10 image-target pairs, a very small sample for a worst-case robustness claim. 3) Latency is high: ~15 s/image worst case (5 s generation + 10 s similarity), reducible to ~1.2 s only by cutting T2I steps 50->10 'with a little compromise in detection performance' (not quantified in main text). 4) The method presumes the victim produces a semantically meaningful caption and that T2I regeneration preserves clean-image semantics; failure cases from T2I variability are acknowledged only qualitatively. 5) Accuracy is reported as the operating metric but AUROC/FPR-at-fixed-TPR are not given in the main tables, and tau requires per-model calibration. 6) Effectiveness partly rests on security-through-stochasticity (search-space inflation), whose guarantees against a compute-unbounded adversary are empirical, not certified.

datasets_used: ImageNet-1K validation images (AttackVLM targeted attacks; also adaptive-attack image-target pairs)
MS-COCO captions (randomly selected as targeted-attack targets)
34,000 clean ImageNet paired zVLM/zT2I feature representations (to train the adaptive-attack Adapter)
Evaluation set of 2000 images per attack (1000 clean + 1000 adversarial), 3 runs
quantitative_results: Detection accuracy up to 99%; overall range ~65-99% across victims/attacks (Table 2)
Table 3: UniDiffuser/AttackVLM-Q Stochastic-MC = 0.98 (>0.13 over next-best baseline; DPS=0.85)
Table 3 weakness: MiniGPT-4/AttackVLM-T MC = 0.66, Stochastic-MC = 0.67 vs CIDER/JailGuard/DPS = 0.85 (Naive = 0.57 here)
Table 3 weakness: Img2Prompt/AttackVLM-T MC = 0.79 vs Stochastic-MC = 0.90

Adaptive attack (BPDA+EoT, eps=8, 100 PGD steps): with 3 encoders ~95% at low/med OTU noise (5e-6, 5e-4); >=7 encoders exceed 98% for all configs; at 1e-3 noise accuracy rises 71% (1 encoder) -> 99% (10 encoders)

Table 1: clean-image similarity typically ~0.62-0.74 (up to ~0.82 for LLaVA/OpenFlamingo) vs adversarial ~0.29-0.57 (up to ~0.72 for Attack-MMFM on LLaVA/OpenFlamingo); the clean-vs-adversarial gap enables detection
Moderate OTU noise (scaling factor <= 5e-4) works best; encoder benefit plateaus after 5-7 encoders (non-adaptive)
Latency ~15 s/image worst case (0.2 s caption + 5 s generation + 10 s similarity), reducible to ~1.2 s by using 10 instead of 50 T2I timesteps; NVIDIA Quadro RTX A6000 48GB
baselines_compared: Multimodal/VLM-specific: CIDER, Naive (5-VLM ensemble voting), CLIP-similarity, JailGuard, SmoothVLM, DPS
Own variants compared: Vanilla MirrorCheck (MC) and Stochastic MirrorCheck (Stochastic-MC)
